results.name: InitStory
results.vision: The first AI-powered storytelling platform on Initia where users generate on-chain narratives and evolving NFTs using Move VM with zero-friction Auto-signing UX.
results.project_description:
## ✦ InitStory — AI-Powered On-Chain Storytelling

**The first storytelling platform on Initia** where every story you write becomes an evolving NFT on a dedicated Move VM appchain.

---

## 🎯 The Problem

While 60%+ of Web3 projects build repetitive DeFi tools, there is zero creative AI content ownership infrastructure on Initia. Users have no way to own their creative output on-chain with a frictionless experience.

## 💡 The Solution

InitStory combines AI generation with Move VM to create a seamless creative ownership experience:

- **Write** a prompt (3 words is enough)

- **AI generates** a full story + unique illustration via DGrid Gateway

- **Mint** it as an NFT on initstory-1 appchain

- **Character evolves** — XP system, levels, on-chain progression

## ⚡ Initia-Native Features

**Auto-signing** — Zero wallet popups. After one approval, every mint happens silently in the background using InterwovenKit session keys.

**Dedicated Appchain** — initstory-1 runs on Initia's Move VM. Every transaction generates fees that stay within the ecosystem.

## 🔧 Tech Stack

- **Smart Contract**: Move VM (initstory-1 appchain)

- **AI Text**: DGrid AI Gateway → qwen/qwen3.6-plus

- **AI Images**: Pollinations Flux

- **Frontend**: React + Vite + InterwovenKit

- **Wallet**: Ghost Wallet + Auto-signing

## 📊 Live Stats

- ✅ 7+ stories minted on-chain

- ✅ Character evolution system working (XP + Levels)

- ✅ Full end-to-end flow: Generate → Preview → Mint

- ✅ Story Gallery with on-chain data

## 🚀 Post-Hackathon Vision

1. Mainnet deployment on Initia

2. Public storytelling platform — any user can create

3. Story marketplace — trade your narrative NFTs

4. Collaborative stories — multiple authors, one NFT
results.team_description: Solo developer. Full-stack Web3 builder with experience in Move VM, React, and AI integration. Built InitStory end-to-end: smart contract, AI backend, and frontend — independently within the hackathon timeframe.

results.name: REX
results.vision: Chess has no trustless record -- outcomes are disputed, wagers rely on trust, platforms own your history. REX fixes this: every move a signed tx, every result permanent and it is on Initia Blockchain. A 1,500-year-old game finally has a ledger.
results.project_description:
REX: 1st 3D Chess on Initia
The world's first cinematic, fully on-chain chess arena — built as a sovereign Initia appchain.

[💻 GitHub](https://github.com/dexarxbt/Rex)  ▶️[YouTube](https://https://youtu.be/vtsuplT9tAA?si=pt4uwUQ-QTw7g6hz) 

**THE PROBLEM**

Chess has been played for over 1,500 years. Every grandmaster move is documented. Every game is analyzed. Yet the game itself — its outcomes, its wagers, its history — has always lived on servers someone else owns.

Online chess today is broken at the infrastructure level, not in gameplay, but in trust and ownership. Match outcomes live in centralized databases that can be altered or taken offline. Wagers pass through third-party platforms that hold custody and charge fees. Tournament results are administered by humans who can make errors or act dishonestly. Replays depend on a company's server staying alive — when it shuts down, history disappears.

The tools exist. The infrastructure exists. What has been missing is a protocol that ties it all together and puts ownership back in the hands of the players.

That is REX.

**WHAT IS REX?**

REX is the world's first cinematic, fully on-chain chess game deployed as a sovereign Minitia L2 appchain on Initia's Interwoven Stack.

Every move is a signed transaction. Every match outcome is a permanent ledger record. Every wager is held in non-custodial smart contract escrow. Board state is derived directly from chain state — it cannot be corrupted by a bad actor or an offline server.

This is not a chess UI bolted onto a blockchain. The blockchain is the game engine.

**HOW IT WORKS**
Three Move smart contracts form the core protocol.
chess.move encodes the complete chess ruleset as formally safe, deterministic resource transitions. The entire 8x8 board is a typed Move resource. Every legal move — castling, en passant, all four promotion variants, check detection, stalemate, and threefold repetition — is validated on-chain before acceptance. Move's resource model makes this uniquely natural: a Piece is a typed resource, not an integer in a mapping. It can be moved, captured, and promoted with the same formal safety guarantees that govern token transfers. A temporal replay index stores board snapshots keyed by move number, so any match in history can be reconstructed and scrubbed at any point — from the ledger alone, not a database.
tournament.move handles bracket construction, round progression, entry fee collection, and prize distribution entirely on-chain. No administrator can alter a result. Prize release is deterministic and automatic. The tournament cannot be cancelled, delayed, or manipulated by any party, including the deployer.
wager.move allows players to stake native INIT tokens in non-custodial escrow before the first move. On checkmate or resignation, the contract releases funds to the winner automatically. On draw, escrow splits equally. No third party touches the funds at any point. Zero principal risk. Zero settlement failure.

**THE ENGINE UNDER THE HOOD
The On-Chain Ruleset**
​Every edge case in chess is enforced by the contract. Castling tracks king and rook move history as resource flags and validates attacked squares via bitboard. En passant logs pawn double-pushes per turn, and the capture window expires after one half-move. Promotion supports all four variants — queen, rook, bishop, and knight — with each piece minted as a new resource on promotion. Check detection crosses the king position against all opponent attack vectors before any move commits. Stalemate computes legal move count and triggers a draw when zero legal moves exist and the king is not in check. Threefold repetition logs a board hash per move and triggers a draw claim on the third match.

**The 3D Arena**

Every chess piece is generated in the browser at runtime. A 2D Staunton profile curve is defined per piece type. LatheGeometry rotates the curve 360 degrees around the Y-axis to produce the base body and stem. ExtrudeGeometry adds piece-specific detail — the cross finial, bishop mitre cut, knight head. PBR materials apply roughness, ambient occlusion, and environment reflections. There are no .glb or .obj files to download. Zero load time. Infinite zoom. Dynamic level of detail based on device capability.

The Kinetic Camera

The camera is never static. It uses quaternion slerp to arc smoothly between orbital positions driven by live match state — active player side, material imbalance, king in check, move execution. The result is broadcast-quality cinematic motion. The blockchain is abstracted entirely. The player sees cinema, not infrastructure.

**BUILT ON INITIA, BUILT FOR INITIA
Auto-Signing via InterwovenKit**

The defining UX failure of on-chain games is the wallet interruption. Requiring a signature popup for every move breaks immersion and makes real-time play impossible. REX solves this at the protocol level using Initia's native auto-signing via InterwovenKit.

Before a match begins, the player grants one scoped session approval. Every subsequent move for the entire match is submitted as a background transaction with zero wallet popups, completing in sub-second time. The session is strictly scoped to the REX contract address, time-limited, and revocable from the wallet at any moment. Zero additional permissions are granted.

Without auto-signing, every move demands a 2-second wallet interruption — minutes of friction per game. With REX auto-signing, one approval covers the entire match. Every move after that is instant and invisible. A fully on-chain game that feels identical to a traditional web game.

**.init Username Resolution**

Raw wallet addresses are a barrier for anyone not already deep in crypto. REX lets players find and challenge opponents by their .init handle instead of a 42-character hex string. Finding a match looks exactly like finding a friend.

**Interwoven Bridge**

REX lives on rex-1, its own Minitia L2. Bridging INIT from L1 to rex-1 happens entirely within the frontend — no external bridge site, no multi-tab flow. OPinit executor and IBC relayer handle cross-chain settlement silently. Players fund their wallet, enter a match, and play. That is it.

**COMPLETE FEATURE SET**

Complete On-Chain Ruleset — Castling, en passant, all four promotion variants enforced by Move contract. No client-side authority over legality.

Temporal Replay Engine — Full 3D board reconstruction at any point in match history. Scrub forward, pause, and step through any line — derived from the ledger, not a database.

Sovereign Tournament Module — Bracket construction, entry fees, round progression, and prize distribution entirely on-chain. No human administrator.

Wagered Protocol — Players stake native tokens before the first move. Non-custodial escrow releases automatically on checkmate, resignation, or draw.

Enshrined Auto-Signing — One session approval. Unlimited moves. Scoped, time-limited, revocable. Zero wallet interruptions mid-game.

Interwoven Bridge — Bridge INIT directly within the frontend. OPinit and IBC handle settlement silently in the background.

Procedural 3D Pieces — All Staunton silhouettes assembled from 2D curves at runtime. No asset files, no load times, infinite zoom.

Cinematic Camera — Quaternion slerp orbital camera driven by match state — material balance, check, active player, move execution.

**TECH STACK**

Smart Contracts — Move (MoveVM) — Chess ruleset, tournaments, escrow, replay index
L2 Rollup — Initia OPinit Stack — Sovereign appchain rex-1, fraud proofs
L1 Security — Initia initiation-2 — Finality, shared liquidity, settlement
Frontend — React + Vite — App routing and state management
3D Renderer — React Three Fiber — Board, pieces, lighting, PBR materials
Wallet and Sessions — InterwovenKit — Auto-signing, bridge, wallet connection
Cross-chain — IBC + OPinit — L1 to L2 asset movement and relaying
JS SDK — initia.js — Transaction construction and broadcast
3D Geometry — LatheGeometry / ExtrudeGeometry — Procedural Staunton pieces, no asset files

**WHAT COMES NEXT**

Immediate: Live deployment, open match lobbies, public replay explorer.

Short term: Ranked ELO system on-chain, spectator mode with live broadcast, custom time controls per match.

Medium term: Mobile-first experience, multi-chain wager support, grandmaster replay library.

Long term: AI opponent module on-chain, DAO-governed tournament formats, REX as a gaming infrastructure layer for other titles.

Payroll infrastructure has not been reimagined in decades. Chess infrastructure has not been reimagined in centuries. REX is the start of that reimagination.

**TEAM**

I built REX entirely alone — every Move contract, every line of 3D rendering code, the auto-signing integration, the cinematic camera system. No co-founders, no contractors. Just weeks of going deep and not coming up for air. I loved every second of it, even the parts that broke me a little.

Initia made this possible as a solo builder. The Interwoven Stack gave me infrastructure that would normally take a whole team to wrangle. I came for the tech and stayed for the community. The people building around Initia genuinely care, and that energy kept me going on the nights where nothing was working. This project has my blood and sweat in it. I am proud of what it became and even more excited about where it goes from here.

dexarxbt — Founder and Solo Developer
[X](https://x.com/dexarxbt)
[GitHub](https://github.com/dexarxbt/Rex)
[Email](https://dexarxbt@gmail.com)

Chain ID: rex-1   VM: MoveVM   L1: Initia initiation-2

Built on the Interwoven Stack — INITIATE Season 1
results.team_description:
I built REX alone. Every single line — the Move contracts, the 3D renderer, the auto-signing integration, the camera system — all of it was me. No co-founders, no contractors, just weeks of going deep and not coming up for air 😃.

Honestly, I loved every second of it. Even the parts that broke me a little. There is something about being so locked into a build that you stop thinking about anything else — that flow state where the problem and the solution start to feel like the same thing. REX put me in that place for weeks and I would not trade it.

Initia is the reason this was even possible as a solo builder. The Interwoven Stack gave me everything I needed to spin up a sovereign appchain, handle cross-chain bridging, and implement auto-signing natively — infrastructure that would normally take a whole team to wrangle. I came for the tech and stayed for the community. The people building around Initia genuinely care, and that energy kept me going on the nights where nothing was working.

This project has my blood and sweat in it. I am proud of what it became and even more excited about where it goes from here.

results.name: IniPoker
results.vision: Online poker today relies on centralized operators who control the deck, hold player funds, and gate withdrawals behind KYC. INIPoker puts every part of the game — dealing, betting, payouts — on-chain, with provable fairness via Band VRF.
results.project_description:
## What is INIPoker?

INIPoker is a fully on-chain Texas Hold'em poker dApp built on Initia L2 (MiniEVM). Every part of the game — dealing cards, betting rounds, showdown evaluation, and payouts — runs inside smart contracts. No centralized backend holds player funds, no operator controls the deck.

## The problem

Traditional online poker rooms have three structural issues:

- **Centralized custody** — operators hold player funds, often gating withdrawals behind KYC and approval queues

- **Unverifiable randomness** — players have to trust the operator's RNG; there's no way to audit if a deck was honestly shuffled

- **No game history transparency** — past hands are stored on private servers, can be edited or deleted

On-chain poker solves all three, but until now suffered from poor UX (Keplr popup on every action, slow state sync, expensive gas).

## How INIPoker solves it

**Provable randomness via Band VRF + commit-reveal**

Before each hand, every player commits a hash of a random salt. The deck is then shuffled using Band VRF as the source of entropy. After the hand, salts are revealed and anyone can audit the deck independently. Neither players, operator, nor the developer can predict or manipulate cards.

**Gasless UX via session wallets**

On first sit-down, an ephemeral keypair is generated in the browser and funded with a small gas reserve. All in-game actions (bet, fold, call, reveal) are signed by this session wallet — no Keplr popups during play. Same UX as a traditional poker client.

**Real-time sync without polling storms**

A lightweight Node.js sync-server polls on-chain state every second and broadcasts it to all connected clients via WebSocket. Eliminates client-client desync and the polling-storm problem common to wagmi/viem-based dApps.

**Polymarket-style fund flow**

Deposit once into the contract → play any number of hands → withdraw whenever. Non-custodial end-to-end. No deposit holds, no withdrawal limits, no frozen accounts.

## Architecture

- **Smart contract layer**: Solidity on MiniEVM L2. Manages tables, sessions, hands, pot, betting rules, hand evaluation

- **Randomness**: Band VRF (with mock provider for testnet)

- **Frontend**: React + viem + wagmi + InterwovenKit (Keplr integration), deployed on Vercel

- **Sync layer**: Node.js WebSocket server polling geth and broadcasting state changes

- **Infrastructure**: MiniEVM L2 node + Caddy (TLS) + sync-server on a single VPS

## What's working today

- Full game cycle: deposit → sit down → multi-hand play → settle → withdraw

- Heads-up (2-player) Texas Hold'em with all betting rounds

- Auto-loop: commits, deals, reveals, evaluations all happen automatically without user clicks

- Cross-client real-time sync via WebSocket

- Verifiable on-chain history of every hand played

## Roadmap

- Six-player tables and sit-and-go tournaments with on-chain prize pools

- Pot Limit Omaha

- Auto-stake idle deposits via Initia DEX integration — passive yield on game balance while waiting for a seat

- Mobile-first redesign

- Tournament leaderboard with NFT achievements

## Why this matters for Initia

Gambling and competitive games are some of the strongest onboarding tools — they predate banking and the internet, and people will always play. Bringing them on-chain with provable fairness and real custody demonstrates what the Initia L2 ecosystem can do beyond DeFi primitives. Real users, real transactions, real fun.

## Tech stack

Solidity · MiniEVM · viem · wagmi · React · Node.js · WebSocket · Band VRF · Foundry · Caddy · Ver
results.team_description:
Solo developer. I'm Mykola — full-stack engineer building INIPoker independently for this hackathon. I designed and implemented all components: smart contracts in Solidity, frontend in React with viem/wagmi, the WebSocket sync-server in Node.js, and the VPS infrastructure (MiniEVM L2 node, Caddy reverse proxy with TLS, systemd services). 

Background in EVM smart contracts and full-stack web development. Previous projects include other dApps deployed on EVM-compatible chains.

results.name: Flowroll
results.vision: Crypto payroll forces a brutal choice: let funds sit idle earning nothing, or take on massive operational risk. Flowroll eliminates that tradeoff, autonomously routing payroll capital across stable pools and returning yield to employers on payday.
results.project_description:
# **Flowroll: The Payroll That Pays Itself**

🔗 [Live Demo](https://youtu.be/nCA1GxCEMrs) | 💻 [GitHub](#https://github.com/LanreAkintayo/flowroll)

---

## The Problem

Crypto payroll forces a brutal choice: let funds sit idle earning nothing, or take on risky complexity to put them to work.

Every payroll cycle, thousands or millions in employer capital waits in a wallet for payday and earns absolutely zero. The money is doing nothing.

Employees have it no better. They are locked into rigid payment cycles that offer zero flexibility. Accessing salary before payday is unnecessarily complex, growing earnings passively requires extra steps most people never take, and moving money to a preferred chain after disbursement is more friction than it should be.

The tools exist. The infrastructure exists. What has been missing is a protocol that ties it all together seamlessly.

That is Flowroll.

---

## What Is Flowroll?

Flowroll is an omnichain, yield-bearing payroll protocol built natively on Initia. It autonomously routes idle employer deposits into risk-adjusted USDC stable pools between deposit and payday, generating yield before a single salary is paid out.

**For employers,** payday stops being just an expense. It becomes a revenue event.

**For employees,** salary stops being an endpoint. It becomes a starting line.

---

## How It Works

**For Employers**

1. Create a payroll group and add employees by their `.init` username or wallet address
2. Set salaries and a payday, then call `setupPayroll()` to fund the cycle
3. Flowroll's yield engine immediately gets to work on the idle capital
4. On payday, every employee is paid in full and on time
5. The yield generated during the cycle flows back to the employer

**No complexity. No disruption. Just capital that works while it waits.**

> Flowroll takes a flat protocol fee strictly from generated yield. The principal is never touched.

---

**For Employees**
Flowroll treats employees as first-class participants, not just recipients:

- **FlowrollCredit:** Request a salary advance before payday with zero approvals or HR hurdles. The advance is automatically deducted from the final payday disbursement.
- **Auto-Save:** When salary lands in `PayVault`, employees can choose to deposit a portion back into a new yield cycle and earn on their own earnings.
- **Bridge Anywhere:** Employees are not stuck on the appchain. Claim salary and bridge it to any chain of choice directly from the Flowroll frontend via the Initia Bridge widget.

---

## The Engine Under the Hood

### 1. Smart Pool Scoring

Idle capital is never deployed blindly. Every registered pool is scored in real time across four dimensions before any deployment decision:

```javascript
Score = APY × LiquidityFactor × RiskMultiplier × ILRiskFactor
```

![flowroll factors.png](https://cdn.dorahacks.io/static/files/19dc17c5fc5131b9a7eb42148d9b4603.png)

The protocol does not just chase the highest APY. It chases the highest *safe* APY given how close payday is.

---

### 2. The Dynamic Buffer Ladder

To guarantee every employee is paid on time without slippage or liquidity crunches, Flowroll runs a 6-tier buffer ladder alongside the scoring engine. As payday approaches, the protocol progressively forces more capital into liquid reserves:

![Screenshot 2026-04-24 225424.png](https://cdn.dorahacks.io/static/files/19dc17cefc3a65046d828ce497ea2224.png)

By the final moments before payday, full liquidity is already guaranteed. Payday never fails.

---

### 3. The Autonomous Yield Agent

An off-chain agent runs continuously and calls `agentRebalance()` at configured intervals. It is stateless and event-driven: it discovers new employer cycles automatically by listening for `CycleStarted` events on-chain. Before broadcasting any transaction, it simulates the rebalance locally — if a cycle is dead or inactive, the revert is caught off-chain, saving gas.

All logic lives on-chain. The agent is purely an executor. The contract responds correctly whenever it is called.

---

### 4. The Adapter Pattern

`YieldRouter` never interacts with pools directly. Every pool has a deployed adapter implementing `IPoolAdapter`. Adding a new pool requires only writing an adapter and calling `addPool()` — zero changes to core routing logic. Switching from testnet mocks to live InitiaDEX pools on mainnet is a single adapter registration.

---

### 5. Atomic Settlement

Settlement on payday happens in a single atomic transaction:

- `YieldRouter` redeems all shares from pools
- Calculates exact yield generated
- Takes the protocol fee from yield only
- Credits every employee's salary to `PayVault` instantly

Zero principal risk. Zero settlement failure.

---

## Built on Initia, Built *for* Initia

Flowroll did not just deploy on Initia. Its architecture was designed around what the Initia ecosystem uniquely offers.

### Auto-Sign (Session Keys)

Without session keys, every payroll action would demand a wallet signature. Flowroll uses Auto-Sign so employers sign once to authorize a session, and the protocol silently handles all batch payroll operations from that point. Employees benefit equally: claiming salary, activating Auto-Save, requesting advances, and repaying debt all happen without repeated wallet prompts. Flowroll feels like a Web2 payroll tool. It runs entirely on-chain.

### `.init` Username Resolution

Raw wallet addresses are a barrier for non-crypto-native HR workflows. Flowroll lets employers add employees by their `.init` handle instead of a 42-character hex address. Onboarding an employee into payroll looks identical to adding them in any traditional HR system.

### Interwoven Bridge

Employees receive salary in `PayVault` on Initia. When they claim, they can bridge their payout to any preferred chain directly from the Flowroll frontend via the Initia bridge widget. The protocol has no dependency on IBC channel IDs or chain-specific token denoms. Bridging lives at the frontend layer, giving employees full control with zero added contract complexity.

> Cross-chain bridging is fully designed at the frontend layer. It will be fully operational on a live Initia testnet or mainnet deployment where IBC channels are active.

---

## Tech Stack

![Screenshot 2026-04-24 225509.png](https://cdn.dorahacks.io/static/files/19dc17d93118d1819ed65c94de69ca0f.png)

---

## What Comes Next

- Full cross-chain bridging shipping immediately once supported by Initia
- Expanded yield strategies across more external protocols
- Custom risk profiles per employer and employee: conservative, balanced, or aggressive
- Native mobile experience for employees to manage salary on the go

---

> Payroll infrastructure has not been reimagined in decades. Flowroll is the start of that reimagination.

> Built for **INITIATE: Initia Hackathon Season 1**
results.team_description: I’m Lanre (Larry Mosh), a Full-Stack Web3 Developer with over four years of experience, and I'm currently building Flowroll solo. For this hackathon, I handled the core development myself, writing the smart contracts with Solidity and Foundry, building the frontend, and coding a custom Node.js agent. While I’m driving the project alone right now to get the infrastructure off the ground, my immediate next step is to bring more talented builders onboard as the protocol scales.

results.name: Zringotts
results.vision: Zringotts is solving the privacy problem in DeFi lending protocols.
results.project_description:
```javascript
# Zringotts


A privacy-preserving lending protocol powered by Zero-Knowledge Proofs on Initia.


## Initia Hackathon Submission


- **Project Name**: Zringotts


### Project Overview


Zringotts is a decentralized lending platform that uses Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZK-SNARKs) to enable private collateralized loans. Users can deposit collateral (WETH), borrow against it (USDC), repay debt, and withdraw collateral - all while keeping their financial positions completely private on-chain. The protocol uses Groth16 proofs generated directly in the browser, combining Circom circuits with EVM smart contracts to create a trustless, privacy-first lending experience.


### Implementation Detail


- **The Custom Implementation**: Zringotts implements a complete ZK lending protocol from scratch, including:
  - Custom Circom circuits for proving valid state transitions (deposit, borrow, repay, withdraw)
  - Merkle tree-based commitment scheme for privacy (only commitments are stored on-chain, not amounts)
  - Interest rate calculations enforced at the circuit level (2% APR for lending, 5% APR for borrowing)
  - LTV (Loan-to-Value) constraints verified in zero-knowledge
  - Browser-based proof generation using snarkjs (15-30 second proving time)
  - Multi-position support with automatic Merkle tree reconstruction from blockchain events


- **The Native Feature**: **Interwoven Bridge** - The frontend implements the Interwoven Bridge to enable cross-chain asset transfers between Initia L1 (initiation-2) and the EVM side (evm-1). Users must bridge INIT tokens from L1 to evm-1 before participating in the lending protocol. The bridge page ([frontend/app/bridge/page.tsx](frontend/app/bridge/page.tsx)) uses `openBridge()` from `@initia/interwovenkit-react` to facilitate seamless cross-chain transfers, leveraging Initia's native Interwoven Bridge infrastructure. This enables users to move assets between the Move and EVM execution environments within the Initia ecosystem.


---


## 🎯 Problem & Solution


### The Problem


**DeFi Lending Lacks Privacy**


Current lending protocols (Aave, Compound, MakerDAO) expose every financial detail on-chain:
- 📊 **Transparent Positions**: Anyone can see exactly how much you've deposited and borrowed
- 🎯 **Easy Target for Attacks**: Large positions become targets for liquidation hunters and front-runners
- 🏢 **Corporate Concerns**: Institutions can't use DeFi without revealing their entire financial strategy to competitors
- 📉 **Market Manipulation**: Whales' positions are public knowledge, enabling predatory trading strategies
- ⚖️ **Regulatory Risk**: Full transparency creates compliance challenges in jurisdictions requiring financial privacy


**Example Scenario:**
```
Alice deposits $1M WETH on Aave
→ Transaction is public
→ Competitors track her position
→ She borrows $400K USDC
→ Price drops slightly
→ Liquidation bots monitor her 24/7
→ She gets liquidated at the worst possible moment
```


**The Core Issue:** Blockchains achieve transparency through full disclosure, but **financial privacy is a fundamental right** that shouldn't be sacrificed for decentralization.


### The Solution


**Zero-Knowledge Proofs Enable Private Lending**


Zringotts solves this by using ZK-SNARKs to prove valid financial operations without revealing the details:


✅ **Private Positions**
- Only commitment hashes are stored on-chain
- Actual deposit/borrow amounts remain encrypted in user's browser
- No one can see your financial position size


✅ **Verifiable Solvency**
- ZK proofs mathematically guarantee you're not over-borrowing
- LTV (Loan-to-Value) constraints enforced in zero-knowledge
- Protocol remains trustless without exposing private data


✅ **Unlinkable Transactions**
- Deposits can't be linked to withdrawals
- Each transaction uses a new commitment with fresh nullifiers
- Even you look like a different user each time


✅ **Competitive Advantage**
- Institutions can use DeFi without revealing strategies
- Individual users protected from front-running and liquidation hunters
- Privacy-by-default, not privacy-by-obscurity


✅ **Regulatory Compliance**
- Users maintain control over their data
- Optional selective disclosure for compliance
- Meets privacy requirements in strict jurisdictions


**Same Scenario with Zringotts:**
```
Alice deposits $1M WETH on Zringotts
→ Only a commitment hash is public: 0x7f3e9a2b...
→ Competitors see: "Someone deposited something"
→ She borrows $400K USDC
→ Only she knows her LTV ratio
→ No one can target her position
→ She manages her collateral privately and safely
```


### Why This Matters


- **$200B+ TVL** in DeFi lending today, all fully transparent
- **Institutional adoption** blocked by privacy concerns
- **User protection** from MEV bots and liquidation snipers
- **Competitive markets** require private information
- **Financial privacy** is a human right, even on-chain


**Zringotts proves you can have both:** trustless verification AND complete privacy.


---


## Architecture Overview


### 🔐 Zero-Knowledge Circuit (`circuits/`)


The heart of Zringotts is a Circom circuit (`zringotts.circom`) that proves valid state transitions without revealing sensitive information.


**Circuit Components:**


1. **Note Structure** (Private)
   - `lendAmt`: Amount of collateral deposited
   - `borrowAmt`: Amount borrowed against collateral
   - `willLiqPrice`: Liquidation price threshold
   - `timestamp`: Last update time
   - `nullifier`: Unique identifier for spending notes
   - `nonce`: Randomness for commitment generation


2. **Key Templates:**
   - `UpdateAmt()`: Calculates interest over time
     ```
     newAmount = (oldAmount × ONE_YEAR + oldAmount × INTEREST_RATE × timeDiff) / ONE_YEAR
     ```
   - `CheckLTV()`: Enforces loan-to-value ratio
     ```
     BORROW_AMT × 100 ≤ 50 × LENT_AMT × WILL_LIG_PRICE
     ```
   - `MerkleTreeInclusionProof()`: Verifies commitment exists in Merkle tree
   - `Main()`: Orchestrates all constraints and public/private inputs


3. **Privacy Guarantees:**
   - Only the commitment hash (`noteHash = Poseidon(lendAmt, borrowAmt, willLiqPrice, timestamp, nullifier, nonce)`) is stored on-chain
   - Actual amounts remain private in user's local storage
   - Nullifiers prevent double-spending
   - Merkle tree hides which position is being updated


**Circuit Parameters:**
- Tree depth: 2 levels (supports 4 positions)
- Hash function: Poseidon (ZK-friendly)
- Proof system: Groth16
- Field size: BN128 curve (~254 bits)


### 📜 Smart Contracts (`contracts/src/`)


**Main Contract: `Zringotts.sol`**


```solidity
contract Zringotts {
    // Privacy-preserving state
    mapping(bytes32 => bool) public commitments;  // Only hashes stored
    mapping(bytes32 => bool) public nullifiers;   // Prevent double-spend
    
    MerkleTreeWithHistory merkleTree;  // LEVEL = 2 (4 leaves)
    
    function deposit(bytes32 commitment, ..., proof) external
    function borrow(bytes32 newCommitment, ..., proof) external
    function repay(bytes32 newCommitment, ..., proof) external
    function withdraw(bytes32 newCommitment, ..., proof) external
}
```


**Key Features:**
- **Groth16Verifier.sol**: On-chain proof verification (auto-generated from circuit)
- **MerkleTreeWithHistory.sol**: Tracks all commitments in a Merkle tree
- **ReentrancyGuard**: Protection against reentrancy attacks
- **MockToken.sol**: WETH and USDC test tokens with minting functionality


**Interest Rates:**
- Lending: 2% APR
- Borrowing: 5% APR
- LTV Threshold: 50% (can borrow up to half of collateral value)


### 🌐 Frontend (`frontend/`)


**Tech Stack:**
- Next.js 15.5.15
- **InterwovenKit 2.x** (Initia Wallet integration)
- Wagmi 2.x (EVM interactions with initiaPrivyWalletConnector)
- Viem (Ethereum utilities)
- snarkjs (browser-based proof generation)
- circomlibjs (Poseidon hashing)


**Key Components:**
- `NewPositionDialog.tsx`: Create new positions (deposit)
- `RepayWithdrawDialog.tsx`: Manage existing positions (borrow/repay/withdraw)
- `PositionSection.tsx`: Display all user positions
- `lib/zkproof.ts`: ZK proof generation logic
- `lib/merkle.ts`: Merkle tree reconstruction from events
- `lib/contracts.ts`: Contract ABIs and addresses
- `lib/wagmi.ts`: Wagmi config with initiaPrivyWalletConnector
- `app/providers.tsx`: InterwovenKitProvider setup


---


## Complete User Flow


### 📊 Step-by-Step Walkthrough


#### 1. **Initial Setup**
```
User connects wallet → Switch to Initia testnet (Chain ID: 2124225178762456)
```


#### 2. **Mint Test Tokens**
```
Account Section → Mint 10 WETH
Status: User now has 10 WETH balance
```


#### 3. **Deposit (Create Position)**
```
Action: Deposit 10 WETH
Frontend:
  ├─ Generate random nullifier and nonce
  ├─ Create note: (10 WETH, 0 USDC, 0 willLiqPrice, timestamp, nullifier, nonce)
  ├─ Compute noteHash = Poseidon(note fields)
  ├─ Generate ZK proof (~20 seconds)
  └─ Submit to contract


Contract:
  ├─ Verify proof
  ├─ Check timestamp (within 5 minutes)
  ├─ Transfer 10 WETH from user
  ├─ Insert noteHash into Merkle tree at index 0
  └─ Emit CommitmentAdded(noteHash, 0)


Result: Position created at merkleIndex = 0
Storage: noteHash stored in localStorage for later use
```


#### 4. **Borrow Against Collateral**
```
Action: Click position → Select "Borrow" → Enter 10 USDC
Frontend:
  ├─ Load old note from localStorage
  ├─ Query Merkle tree commitments from blockchain
  ├─ Compute Merkle path for position at index 0
  ├─ Create new note: (10 WETH, 10 USDC, 1000000 willLiqPrice, same timestamp, new nullifier, new nonce)
  ├─ Check LTV: 10 USDC × 100 ≤ 50 × 10 WETH × 1000000 ✓
  ├─ Generate ZK proof with:
  │   • Old note (private)
  │   • New note (private)
  │   • Merkle proof (public root, private path)
  │   • Borrow amount: +10 USDC
  └─ Submit to contract


Contract:
  ├─ Verify proof
  ├─ Check Merkle root is valid
  ├─ Mark old nullifier as spent
  ├─ Transfer 10 USDC to user
  ├─ Insert new noteHash into Merkle tree at index 1
  └─ Emit CommitmentAdded(newNoteHash, 1)


Result: Position updated to merkleIndex = 1
Storage: New note stored in localStorage, replacing old one
Balance: User has 10 WETH (locked) + 10 USDC (borrowed)
```


#### 5. **Repay Debt**
```
Action: Click position → Select "Repay" → Enter 10 USDC
Frontend:
  ├─ Load current note from localStorage (has 10 WETH, 10 USDC)
  ├─ Query Merkle tree for index 1
  ├─ Create new note: (10 WETH, 0 USDC, 1000000 willLiqPrice, same timestamp, new nullifier, new nonce)
  ├─ Generate ZK proof with:
  │   • Borrow amount: -10 USDC (repayment)
  └─ Submit to contract


Contract:
  ├─ Verify proof
  ├─ Transfer 10 USDC from user back to contract
  ├─ Mark nullifier as spent
  ├─ Insert new noteHash at index 2
  └─ Emit CommitmentAdded(newNoteHash, 2)


Result: Position updated to merkleIndex = 2
Storage: Updated note (debt = 0)
Balance: Debt cleared, collateral still locked
```


#### 6. **Withdraw Collateral**
```
Action: Click position → Select "Withdraw" → Enter 10 WETH
Frontend:
  ├─ Load current note (10 WETH, 0 USDC)
  ├─ Query Merkle tree for index 2
  ├─ Create new note: (0 WETH, 0 USDC, 1000000 willLiqPrice, same timestamp, new nullifier, new nonce)
  ├─ Generate ZK proof with:
  │   • Lend amount: -10 WETH (withdrawal)
  └─ Submit to contract


Contract:
  ├─ Verify proof
  ├─ Transfer 10 WETH back to user
  ├─ Mark nullifier as spent
  ├─ Insert new noteHash at index 3
  └─ Emit CommitmentAdded(newNoteHash, 3)


Result: Position updated to merkleIndex = 3
Storage: Updated note (fully withdrawn)
Balance: 10 WETH returned to user, position closed
```


---


## Privacy Model


### What's Private:
- ✅ Deposit amounts
- ✅ Borrow amounts  
- ✅ Position ownership
- ✅ Transaction history (can't link deposits to withdrawals)


### What's Public:
- ❌ Merkle root (proves a commitment exists)
- ❌ Nullifiers (prevents double-spending)
- ❌ Proof itself (but doesn't reveal private inputs)


### Security Features:
- **Commitment Scheme**: Poseidon hash binds all note fields
- **Nullifiers**: Each note can only be spent once
- **Merkle Tree**: Hides which specific position is being updated
- **Zero-Knowledge**: Amounts never appear on-chain


---


## How to Run Locally


### Prerequisites
```bash
Node.js >= 18
Foundry (forge, anvil)
pnpm or npm
```


### 1. Clone and Install
```bash
git clone <your-repo-url>
cd Zringotts
cd frontend && pnpm install
cd ../contracts && forge install
```


### 2. Compile Circuit (Optional - already compiled)
```bash
cd circuits
npm install
make  # Compiles circuit and generates verification key
```


### 3. Deploy Contracts
```bash
cd contracts
# Set your private key
export PRIVATE_KEY="0x..."


# Deploy to Initia testnet
forge script script/DeployNoOracle.s.sol:DeployNoOracle \
  --rpc-url https://jsonrpc-evm-1.anvil.asia-southeast.initia.xyz \
  --broadcast --legacy
```


### 4. Update Frontend Config
Edit `frontend/lib/contracts.ts` with your deployed addresses:
```typescript
export const ZRINGOTTS_ADDRESS = '0x...'
export const WETH_ADDRESS = '0x...'
export const USDC_ADDRESS = '0x...'
```


### 5. Add Liquidity (for borrowing)
```bash
cast send $USDC_ADDRESS "mint(address,uint256)" \
  $ZRINGOTTS_ADDRESS 10000000000 \
  --rpc-url https://jsonrpc-evm-1.anvil.asia-southeast.initia.xyz \
  --private-key $PRIVATE_KEY --legacy
```


### 6. Run Frontend
```bash
cd frontend
npm run dev
```
Open http://localhost:3000


### 7. Test the Full Flow
1. Connect wallet (MetaMask/Keplr)
2. Switch to Initia testnet
3. **Mint 10 WETH** in Account section
4. **Deposit 10 WETH** → creates position (index 0)
5. **Borrow 10 USDC** → updates to index 1 ✅
6. **Repay 10 USDC** → updates to index 2 ✅
7. **Withdraw 10 WETH** → updates to index 3 ✅


---


## Technical Highlights


### Circuit Optimization
- **Timestamp Hack**: Uses same timestamp for updates to avoid division remainder issues in field arithmetic
- **Bit Ordering**: LSB-first for Merkle index bits to match Circom expectations
- **Interest Calculation**: Done in-circuit to enforce protocol rules


### Frontend Optimizations
- **Event-Based Merkle Reconstruction**: Queries `CommitmentAdded` events to rebuild tree state
- **Automatic Position Updates**: After each transaction, updates localStorage with new commitment and merkleIndex
- **Transaction Receipt Verification**: Waits for confirmation before updating UI


### Known Limitations
- Tree depth limited to 2 (4 max positions per user)
- Interest only calculated when timestamps differ (workaround: reuse old timestamp)
- Proof generation takes 15-30 seconds (client-side computation)
- No liquidation mechanism implemented (only LTV checks)


---


## Deployed Contracts (Latest)


**Initia EVM-1 Testnet:**
- Zringotts: `0x510BF2877a200b5aE58a327A442638817B88C5dE`
- WETH: `0xc5a4BCea0AB9f86CA1b1502aB9D7b3224EB221ea`
- USDC: `0xCBA2A1554C350AEcaA311917aEf0F7C266600BdA`
- Verifier: `0xdffCbf3c0206375979980E5C4E6D582aAe13ffa9`


**Network Details:**
- Chain ID: `2124225178762456`
- RPC: `https://jsonrpc-evm-1.anvil.asia-southeast.initia.xyz`


---


## Project Structure


```
Zringotts/
├── circuits/               # Zero-knowledge circuits
│   ├── src/
│   │   └── zringotts.circom       # Main circuit
│   ├── build/
│   │   ├── zringotts.wasm         # Compiled circuit
│   │   ├── zringotts.zkey         # Proving key
│   │   └── Groth16Verifier.sol    # Verifier contract
│   └── Makefile
│
├── contracts/              # Solidity smart contracts
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── Zringotts.sol          # Main lending contract
│   │   ├── MerkleTreeWithHistory.sol
│   │   ├── Groth16Verifier.sol
│   │   └── MockToken.sol
│   └── script/
│       └── DeployNoOracle.s.sol
│
├── frontend/               # Next.js frontend
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── page.tsx               # Main UI
│   │   └── providers.tsx          # Wagmi config
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── NewPositionDialog.tsx  # Deposit
│   │   ├── RepayWithdrawDialog.tsx # Borrow/Repay/Withdraw
│   │   └── PositionSection.tsx
│   ├── lib/
│   │   ├── zkproof.ts             # Proof generation
│   │   ├── merkle.ts              # Tree reconstruction
│   │   ├── contracts.ts           # ABIs & addresses
│   │   └── wagmi.ts               # Wallet config
│   └── public/
│       ├── zringotts.wasm
│       └── zringotts.zkey
│
└── .initia/
    └── submission.json     # Hackathon submission
```


---


## Future Improvements


- [ ] Implement liquidation mechanism with oracle integration
- [ ] Support multiple collateral types (ETH, BTC, etc.)
- [ ] Increase tree depth for unlimited positions
- [ ] Add withdrawal rate limits for security
- [ ] Implement circuit-based interest calculation that works with any timestamp
- [ ] Optimize proof generation (currently 15-30s)
- [ ] Add relayer support for gas-less transactions
- [ ] Multi-sig governance for protocol parameters


---


## License


MIT


---


## Team


Built for Initia Hackathon 2026


---


## Acknowledgments


- Circom & snarkjs by iden3
- Tornado Cash for Merkle tree inspiration
- Initia for the Interwoven infrastructure
- OpenZeppelin for secure contract libraries


```

results.name: Initnomo
results.vision: The first binary options trading dApp on Blockchain where users can trade 1000+ Crypto, Stocks, Forex, Metals and Commodities in 5s-1m time-charts.
results.project_description:
## Story & Inspiration

In 2021, I saw an advertisement of a forex binary options app called Binomo. It was a mobile app promoted by big influencers. I tried the free (paper) mode and made 10× in a week. Then I put in three months of my savings in real mode — and lost it all.

Later I found Reddit threads showing that Binomo ran on algorithms that let users win in trial mode and lose in real mode. This happened to millions of traders worldwide.

That day I decided to build a transparent, oracle-driven binary options platform. But in 2021, sub-1-second price feeds didn't exist in Web3. I waited 5 years, and in 2026 the tools were finally ready. **Initnomo** is the result.

---

## The Problem

Today, Binary options trading in Web3 doesn't exist at all.

- Web2 platforms (Binomo, IQ Option) are opaque, algorithmically biased, and fraudulent.
- There were no real-time data oracles that could deliver price feeds in under 1 second — until Pyth Hermes.
- We have 590 million crypto users and 400 million transactions happens every single day on top 200 blockchains.
- One big news/ One big move → oracle crash → no fast-settlement dapp can function.

---

## The Solution

Solution - Initnomo

- Where every millisecond is tracked by Pyth oracles to pull real-time data
- Traders can Trade over 300+ Crypto, 100+ Stocks, 5+ Metals, 10+Forex, Commodities, Bonds every single thing on Real-time price chart
- Users can bet unlimited times without signing txn
- Every user can trade binary options at lightning speed at 5s,10s,15s,30s,1m timeframes
- Infinite txns, no cap amounts and One Single Treasury
- Plan is to introduce 1-10x leverage, trade in open crypto markets freely And Settlement in <0.001 ms
- Like Binomo of Web2, but 10x better than any other dapp in existence.

---

## Platform Features

### Deposits

- User sends **INIT** on **Initia mainnet** to the treasury (`NEXT_PUBLIC_INITIA_TREASURY_ADDRESS`); ensure `NEXT_PUBLIC_INITIA_CHAIN_ID=interwoven-1` in production
- Server verifies the transaction with Initia mainnet REST/RPC (`verifyInitiaDepositTx`, `lib/initia/`)
- Platform fee deducted; **net** amount credited to `user_balances` via `update_balance_for_deposit`
- Fee logged as `platform_fee` in `balance_audit_log`

### Withdrawals

- User requests withdrawal amount ≤ available **INIT** house balance
- Server uses `transferINITFromTreasury` to send **net INIT** to the user’s Initia address (see `app/api/balance/withdraw/route.ts`)
- **Withdrawal cap / review thresholds** may still apply depending on your Supabase policies and ops playbooks
- Platform fee collected via `collectPlatformFeeFromTreasury`; treasury sends **net** on-chain; Supabase balance updated

### Balance System

- Every wallet has one row per currency in `user_balances`
- All bet deductions and win credits happen atomically via Supabase RPCs
- Full audit trail in `balance_audit_log` with `operation_type`: `deposit`, `withdrawal`, `bet_placed`, `bet_won`, `platform_fee`, `admin_balance_wipe`

### Player Tiers

TierDeposit/Withdrawal Fee`free`10%`standard`9%`vip`8%

### Referrals

- Every user gets a referral code
- Referrer earns credit when referee deposits; tracked in `user_referrals`
- Admin dashboard shows referral leaderboard

### Access Codes

- Invite-only onboarding via access codes stored in `access_codes` table
- Admin can generate batches and export CSV

### Waitlist

- Email capture at `/waitlist`
- Admin dashboard shows full list

### Public Live Stats

- `GET /api/stats/public` serves aggregate stats (total bets, volume, payouts, win rate, unique wallets)
- Displayed on the landing page in `LiveStatsSection` with animated counters

### Session Tracking

- `POST /api/session/ping` keeps dwell-time alive
- `DELETE /api/session/ping` ends session
- Stored in `user_sessions` for admin Wallet Intel

### Leaderboard

- `GET /api/bets/leaderboard` — aggregated per-wallet profit + win rate
- 60s in-memory cache; 503 fallback to cached snapshot on timeout

### Deduplication & Race Condition Prevention

- `bet_id` checked against `balance_audit_log` before each payout — duplicate credits blocked server-side
- Client-side: `resolveBet` in Zustand removes the bet from `activeBets` synchronously before any async API call
- `userAddress` captured at bet placement time so settlement works even if wallet disconnects mid-round

## Monetization

The platform earns in two ways:

### 1. Platform Fees (Deposit + Withdrawal)

10% / 9% / 8% of every on-chain deposit and withdrawal depending on user tier. Fee is transferred from the treasury to the platform fee collector wallet on every transaction.

### 2. Blitz entry fees

One-time per-window fee (see [Blitz entry fee (Initia / INIT)](https://github.com/0xamaan-dev/Initnomo#blitz-entry-fee-initia--init)); collected to the configured **INIT** platform fee wallet when set.

Estimated earnings (best-effort):

Monthly bet volume. Expected monthly earnings range. Notes:

- About $5M, About $100k to $250k per month. Based on withdrawal take rate plus house edge model assumptions.
- About $20M, about $400k to $1.0M per month. Depends on cash-out behaviour and effective fee utilisation

---

## Roadmap

### Phase 0 — Stability & Hardening ✅

- Core reliability on `/trade`
- Admin dashboard + analytics
- Multi-chain deposit/withdrawal
- Fee system + audit trail

### Phase 1 — Controlled Beta

- Limited user cohort
- Monitor: price feed timeouts, settlement correctness, withdrawal success rate
- Tighten incident response loop

### Phase 2 — Public Launch

- Open access progressively after beta thresholds
- Scale APIs and observability
- Community growth: X/Twitter, Telegram, Discord, referral campaigns

### Phase 3 — Chain Expansion

- Add chains incrementally (one at a time)
- Deposit/withdraw reconciliation per new chain
- Maintain consistent security and payout standards

### Product Roadmap

- **P2P Mode** — shift Classic from P2T (person-to-treasury) to P2P to reduce house risk
- **200+ new assets** — more crypto, forex, stocks, commodities via Pyth
- **Leverage 1–100×** *(optional)*
- **Trader profiles** — highest P&L, most accurate, biggest risk taker
- **Tournaments** *(optional)*
- **Social trading** — follow traders, copy trades, leaderboards *(optional)*
- **Mobile-first redesign**
- **Top 50 chain expansion** *(subject to adoption checks)*

### Launch Gates (per phase)

- 0 critical security findings open
- Build and TypeScript checks passing
- Settlement and balance invariants verified
- Incident playbook and rollback path confirmed

## Future

Endless possibilities: anything to everything related to P2P mode: Stocks, Forex, Options, Derivatives, Futures, DEX

Ultimate Objective: To be the next PolyMarket for Binary Options Predictions

results.name: Initia MInd
results.vision: To build an intent-centric execution layer that bridges natural language input with autonomous on-chain transactions.
results.project_description:
## **Project Overview: InitiaMind**

**InitiaMind** is a next-generation, terminal-grade AI command center specifically engineered for the **Initia** ecosystem. It bridges the gap between complex blockchain infrastructure and user intent by providing a high-performance, minimalist interface for "Intent-to-Onchain" automation.

### **The Problem**

Traditional Web3 interfaces are often cluttered, overwhelming for power users, and fragmented across multiple chains and protocols. For a developer-centric network like Initia, users need a tool that matches the network's speed and modularity without the friction of traditional UI components.

### **The Solution**

InitiaMind transforms natural language into executable blockchain logic. By combining a **strict terminal aesthetic** with **Agentic AI**, it allows users to manage their assets on the **initiation-2** testnet through simple commands, bypassing manual form-filling and complex navigation.

### **Key Technical Features**

- **Intent-Based Execution:** Advanced natural language parsing (NLP) that identifies user goals—such as swapping, staking, or providing liquidity—and maps them to specific smart contract interactions.
- **Initia Native Integration:** Built on the **OPinit Stack**, supporting real-time transaction broadcasting to the `initiation-2` network via **Keplr** wallet.
- **AI Guardian System:** A built-in risk layer that provides pre-flight simulations, 30-day performance projections, and confidence scores before any transaction is signed.
- **Modular Architecture:** Leveraging Initia's interoperability to handle assets across different "Minitias" (appchains) within a single command stream.
- **Real-time Execution Logs:** A live, developer-grade terminal feed that tracks the transaction lifecycle: `Intent Received` → `Strategy Built` → `Broadcasted` → `Confirmed`.
**Tech Stack**
- **Frontend:** Next.js (React) with a custom monospace terminal UI.
- **Blockchain:** Initia Testnet (`initiation-2`), Cosmos SDK, Keplr Wallet integration.
- **AI Engine:** Intent parsing logic with simulated risk-reward strategy generation.
- **Styling:** Strict Minimalist Black & White (No CSS decoration, focus on raw data).

results.name: Premia
results.vision: An onchain sportsbook / prediction market built as a Move appchain on Initia
results.project_description:
```javascript
# InitBet (Phantasma)


> **A trust-minimized onchain sportsbook built as a Move appchain on Initia**


[![Demo Video](https://img.shields.io/badge/Demo-YouTube-red)](https://youtu.be/KBnWdS_2_Ew)
[![Chain ID](https://img.shields.io/badge/Chain%20ID-movegame--1-blue)]()
[![VM](https://img.shields.io/badge/VM-Move-green)]()


## Overview


InitBet is a fully onchain sportsbook and prediction market where users place **1X2 bets** (Home/Draw/Away) against a shared **House Pool**. Liquidity providers can deposit funds to become the counterparty to every bet, earning yield from losing bets while sharing risk on winning ones.


**Why build a sportsbook on Initia?**


Traditional sports betting platforms suffer from:
- **Opaque odds manipulation** - centralized bookmakers adjust odds without transparency
- **Slow withdrawals** - funds locked in custodial accounts
- **Geographic restrictions** - users excluded based on location
- **High fees** - platform takes 5-15% margins


InitBet solves these by putting everything onchain: odds updates are cryptographically signed, settlements are verifiable, and the house pool is fully transparent.


## Initia-Native Features


| Feature | Implementation |
|---------|---------------|
| **Auto-Signing (Session UX)** | Bettors approve a session key to sign bet transactions without wallet popups. Critical for live betting UX where odds change rapidly. |
| **InterwovenKit** | Full wallet connection via `@initia/interwovenkit-react` with support for Privy wallets, social login, and native Initia wallets. |
| **Interwoven Bridge** | Users can bridge assets from other Initia rollups to fund their betting wallet. |
| **Initia Usernames (.init)** | Display `.init` usernames instead of raw addresses in the UI. |


## Technical Architecture


```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         Frontend (Next.js)                       │
│  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Markets Page│  │ My Bets Page│  │ Account/LP Dashboard    │  │
│  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘  └─────────────────────────┘  │
│                            │                                     │
│              InterwovenKit + Auto-Sign Sessions                  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                             │
                             ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  Initia Move Appchain (movegame-1)              │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │              quadratic_market::sportsbook                │    │
│  │  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  │    │
│  │  │ House Pool  │  │ Bet Slips   │  │ Oracle Verify   │  │    │
│  │  │ (LP Shares) │  │ (Parlays)   │  │ (Ed25519 Sigs)  │  │    │
│  │  └─────────────┘  └─────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                                                  │
│  Native Integrations:                                            │
│  • initia_std::oracle   - Price feeds for settlement             │
│  • initia_std::dex      - Swap any token to betting token        │
│  • initia_std::multisig - Admin can be a multisig                │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```


## Smart Contract Features


The Move contract (`sportsbook.move`) implements:


### Core Betting
- **Single bets** - Bet on one match outcome
- **Parlay bets** - Combine multiple selections for multiplied odds
- **Same-game parlays** - Multiple bets on the same match (with de-duplicated exposure)
- **Live odds updates** - Admin pushes signed odds from off-chain feed


### House Pool (LP)
- **Add liquidity** - Deposit tokens, receive LP shares
- **Withdrawal queue** - Request withdrawal, processed when liquidity available
- **Exposure management** - Per-match exposure caps to limit risk
- **First-depositor protection** - Prevents ERC4626 inflation attacks


### Settlement
- **Signed batch settlement** - Oracle signs match results, verified on-chain
- **Native oracle integration** - Can use `initia_std::oracle` for decentralized settlement
- **Automatic payout calculation** - Winners claim from locked pool


### Security
- Ed25519 signature verification for all odds/settlement updates
- IBC router authentication for cross-chain bets
- Oracle staleness checks (rejects data > 120 seconds old)
- Pause functionality for emergencies


## Demo Video


**Watch the full demo**: [https://youtu.be/KBnWdS_2_Ew](https://youtu.be/KBnWdS_2_Ew)


The demo shows:
1. Connecting wallet via InterwovenKit
2. Enabling Auto-Sign session
3. Browsing live sports markets
4. Placing a bet (with session signing - no popup!)
5. Viewing bet slip and potential payout
6. LP dashboard for liquidity providers


## How to Run


### Prerequisites
- Node.js 18+
- pnpm
- Python 3.8+ (for e2e tests)
- Go 1.22+ (for running local node)


### 1. Start Local Initia Rollup


```bash
# Start the Move VM node
bash start_node.sh


# Endpoints:
# - RPC: http://localhost:26657
# - LCD: http://localhost:1317
# - Chain ID: movegame-1
```


### 2. Deploy & Seed Contract


```bash
# This script:
# - Builds the Move contract
# - Publishes to local chain
# - Adds initial liquidity
# - Creates sample matches
# - Places test bets
python my-initia-project/e2e_test.py
```


### 3. Run Frontend


```bash
cd frontend
pnpm install
pnpm dev
```


Open [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000)


### 4. Connect & Bet


1. Click "Connect Wallet" (uses InterwovenKit)
2. Enable Auto-Sign in wallet settings
3. Browse Markets tab
4. Click odds to add to bet slip
5. Enter stake and place bet


## Project Structure


```
.
├── .initia/
│   └── submission.json          # Hackathon submission metadata
├── my-initia-project/
│   └── quadratic_market/
│       └── sources/
│           └── sportsbook.move  # Core Move contract
├── frontend/
│   ├── app/
│   │   ├── page.tsx             # Main entry
│   │   └── providers.tsx        # InterwovenKit + Wagmi setup
│   ├── components/
│   │   ├── raid-club-app.tsx    # Main app component
│   │   ├── top-nav.tsx          # Navigation + wallet button
│   │   ├── bet-slip-modal.tsx   # Bet placement UI
│   │   └── pages/               # Markets, Live, MyBets, Account
│   └── lib/
│       ├── initia/config.ts     # Chain configuration
│       └── game/store.ts        # Zustand state management
├── start_node.sh                # Script to run local node
└── README.md
```


## Submission Details


| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Project Name** | InitBet |
| **Rollup Chain ID** | `movegame-1` |
| **Deployed Address** | `0x4AC3EF7D007CAEFE44AE2E9F4FD2A057612B206D` |
| **VM** | Move |
| **Native Feature** | Auto-Signing (Session UX) |
| **Demo Video** | [https://youtu.be/KBnWdS_2_Ew](https://youtu.be/KBnWdS_2_Ew) |


## Revenue Model


InitBet captures value through:


1. **Betting margin** - Odds are set with a small house edge (configurable)
2. **LP yield** - House pool earns from losing bets
3. **Transaction fees** - Appchain captures all gas fees (no value leakage to L1)


As an Initia appchain, InitBet keeps 100% of transaction fees rather than paying gas to external validators.


## Future Roadmap


- [ ] Live in-play betting with sub-second odds updates
- [ ] Multiple sports (currently soccer, adding basketball, tennis, esports)
- [ ] Liquidity mining rewards for early LPs
- [ ] Governance token for odds curation
- [ ] Integration with Pyth/Supra for decentralized odds feeds
- [ ] Mobile app with push notifications


## Tech Stack


- **Smart Contract**: Move (Initia MoveVM)
- **Frontend**: Next.js 15, React 19, TailwindCSS
- **Wallet**: InterwovenKit (`@initia/interwovenkit-react`)
- **State**: Zustand, TanStack Query
- **Chain**: Initia Move Appchain


## License


MIT


---


Built for **INITIATE: The Initia Hackathon (Season 1)**

results.name: Ghost Yield
results.vision: Our vision is to move DeFi from manual yield farming to autonomous finance. Ghost Yield uses Initia-native automation to let agents handle repetitive actions like compounding, reducing friction, improving capital efficiency, and simplifying UX.
results.project_description:
# Ghost Yield

## Overview

Ghost Yield is a DeFi vault built on Initia that demonstrates **automated compounding through Ghost Operators**.

In most DeFi protocols, users must return repeatedly to monitor positions and manually compound yield. This creates friction, reduces efficiency, and makes the overall experience feel active and operational rather than seamless.

Ghost Yield solves this by introducing **Ghost Operators**: automated actors that can trigger compounding on behalf of users using **Initia-native automation concepts**, especially session keys and auto-signing flows. Instead of the user manually managing every repetitive step, the system is designed so yield can be rolled back into principal with minimal user intervention.

This project is a practical demonstration of how Initia can enable a new class of **autonomous, agent-assisted DeFi applications**.

---

## Problem

Traditional yield farming has a UX problem.

Even when the core financial primitive is valuable, users often need to:

- deposit assets manually

- keep checking their position

- come back again and again to compound rewards

- actively manage the vault to maximize returns

- remember operational steps that are easy to miss

This creates three major issues:

1. **Inefficiency**  

   Uncompounded rewards sit idle until the user returns.

2. **Poor user experience**  

   Users must repeatedly interact with the protocol for routine maintenance.

3. **Reduced accessibility**  

   DeFi becomes more suitable for highly active users and less friendly for normal users who want simpler capital growth.

Ghost Yield addresses this by shifting DeFi from **manual maintenance** to **automated execution**.

---

## Solution

Ghost Yield is a vault where users can deposit assets and earn yield over time. The key innovation is that yield compounding does not need to depend on the user manually returning to the app every time.

Instead, **Ghost Operators** can trigger the public compounding mechanism on behalf of users.

The flow is:

1. User connects wallet

2. User mints test MockUSDC

3. User approves the vault

4. User deposits funds into the vault

5. Yield accrues over time

6. A Ghost Operator triggers compounding

7. Pending yield is added back into staked balance

8. User withdraws principal plus earned and compounded yield

This makes the user journey much simpler while still preserving transparent onchain vault behavior.

---

## Why Initia

Ghost Yield is built as an **Initia-native idea**, not just a generic vault deployed on another chain.

The project is centered around Initia’s ability to support:

- appchain-native UX

- session key based flows

- auto-signing patterns

- smoother execution for repetitive actions

- better foundations for agent-assisted onchain applications

The concept of **Ghost Operators** is especially aligned with Initia because Initia opens the door for more seamless and programmable interactions, where repetitive user actions can be delegated to trusted execution flows instead of requiring constant manual wallet signing.

This is what makes Ghost Yield more than a normal vault demo. It is a demonstration of how Initia can support **autonomous DeFi experiences**.

---

## Core Innovation: Ghost Operators

The main innovation in Ghost Yield is the concept of **Ghost Operators**.

Ghost Operators are automated agents or operator flows that handle repetitive vault maintenance tasks, especially compounding.

In a normal DeFi vault:

- the user deposits

- yield accumulates

- the user must return and manually compound

In Ghost Yield:

- the user deposits

- yield accumulates

- a Ghost Operator can trigger compounding on the user’s behalf

- pending yield gets converted into additional principal

This changes DeFi UX in an important way:

- users focus on intent, not repetitive execution

- capital becomes more efficient

- protocol interactions become more autonomous

- the app feels smarter and less operationally heavy

Ghost Yield uses this idea to show how Initia can power applications where agents participate in execution in a meaningful, user-beneficial way.

---

## Key Features

### 1. Wallet Connection

Users connect their wallet to interact with the app and manage their vault position.

### 2. MockUSDC Minting

For demo and testing purposes, users can mint MockUSDC directly from the interface.

### 3. Token Approval

Before depositing, users approve the vault contract to use a chosen amount of MockUSDC.

### 4. Vault Deposit

Users deposit assets into the vault to begin earning yield.

### 5. Live Position Tracking

The interface shows key portfolio information such as:

- wallet balance

- staked balance

- pending yield

### 6. Yield Accrual

Once deposited, the user’s funds begin generating yield over time.

### 7. Public Compound Function

The app exposes a compounding action that can be triggered by a Ghost Operator.

### 8. Automated Compounding Concept

Compounding is modeled as a delegated operational action rather than something the user must repeatedly manage.

### 9. Safe Withdraw Flow

Users can withdraw their principal and earned yield through a simplified withdraw helper.

---

## Detailed User Flow

### Step 1: Connect Wallet

The user connects their wallet to the Ghost Yield frontend.

### Step 2: Mint Test Assets

The user mints **1000 MockUSDC** for testing.

### Step 3: Approve the Vault

The user enters **300 USDC** and approves the vault to use that amount.

### Step 4: Deposit into the Vault

The user deposits **300 USDC** into the vault.

### Step 5: Wait for Yield to Accrue

After deposit, yield starts accumulating over time.

### Step 6: Refresh and Observe State

The user can view:

- current wallet balance

- current staked balance

- pending yield waiting to be compounded

### Step 7: Compound Through Ghost Operator Logic

A Ghost Operator triggers the **Compound Now** function.

As a result:

- pending yield decreases

- staked balance increases

- the user’s capital base grows

### Step 8: Withdraw

The user uses the safe withdraw helper, for example with **Set 95%**, and withdraws funds.

This demonstrates the complete lifecycle of the product.

---

## Demo Flow

The live demo follows this sequence:

1. Connect Wallet

2. Refresh

3. Mint `1000` MockUSDC

4. Approve `300`

5. Deposit `300`

6. Refresh

7. Wait 10–20 seconds

8. Click `Compound Now`

9. Refresh

10. In Withdraw, click `Set 95%`

11. Click `Withdraw`

This flow clearly shows:

- asset creation

- deposit onboarding

- yield generation

- compounding

- withdrawal

---

## Technical Architecture

Ghost Yield consists of three main parts:

### Smart Contracts

The smart contracts manage:

- deposits

- accounting of user balances

- yield accrual logic

- compounding behavior

- withdrawal flow

The vault contract exposes the functionality needed for Ghost Operators to trigger compounding.

### Frontend

The frontend provides:

- wallet connection

- minting interface

- approval and deposit actions

- live display of balances

- compound action

- withdrawal controls

The frontend is designed to make the product understandable and demo-friendly.

### Initia-Native Automation Concept

The system design is based on the idea that Initia’s session key and auto-signing capabilities can enable operator-style execution for repetitive DeFi actions.

Even in demo form, this establishes the design pattern for agent-assisted DeFi on Initia.

---

## What Makes Ghost Yield Different

There are many vaults in DeFi, but Ghost Yield is focused on a different question:

**How should DeFi feel when repetitive user actions are handled through automation?**

What makes Ghost Yield different is not just the vault itself, but the UX model around it.

### Standard vaults optimize yield mechanics

Ghost Yield also optimizes for **execution experience**

### Standard vaults expect user maintenance

Ghost Yield introduces **operator-assisted maintenance**

### Standard vaults are passive until the user returns

Ghost Yield demonstrates **autonomous compounding behavior**

This makes the project a strong conceptual fit for Initia.

---

## User Value

Ghost Yield provides value in several ways:

### Better UX

Users do not need to repeatedly remember when to compound.

### Better Capital Efficiency

Yield can be folded back into principal more quickly.

### Lower Operational Burden

The system handles repetitive maintenance-style actions.

### Better Accessibility

Users who are less active can still benefit from a more optimized position.

### Better Demonstration of Initia

The project highlights how Initia can support automation-centered onchain products.

---

## Vision

Our vision is to move DeFi from manual yield farming to autonomous finance.

Ghost Yield is an early demonstration of that future. Instead of forcing users to repeatedly return and execute maintenance actions themselves, DeFi applications should increasingly be built around intent-based participation, where users define what they want and the system handles repetitive execution in the background.

We believe Initia is a strong foundation for this future because it enables more seamless, programmable, and automation-friendly onchain experiences.

Ghost Yield is the first step toward a world where DeFi becomes:

- more autonomous

- more intelligent

- more capital efficient

- more user friendly

---

## Future Scope

Ghost Yield can be expanded significantly beyond the current demo.

Possible future directions include:

### Real Scheduled Compounding

Operators or agents can trigger compounding automatically at defined intervals.

### User-Defined Automation Policies

Users can specify preferences for when and how compounding should happen.

### Multi-Vault Strategies

Ghost Operators could route funds across strategies for better returns.

### Yield Optimization Logic

Automation could choose the best compounding timings based on cost and reward conditions.

### Agent Marketplace

A broader ecosystem could emerge where different agents compete to provide automation services.

### Expanded DeFi Operations

The same operator model could be extended beyond compounding to:

- rebalancing

- reward claiming

- rollover strategies

- liquidity migration

- vault maintenance

This would move DeFi closer to fully agent-assisted capital management.

results.name: Griddy: Grid Trading
results.vision: Griddy is a gamified trading platform that utilizes live Bitcoin chart to bet on its prices
results.project_description:
**TL;DR:** Griddy turns BTC price action into a clickable grid. Stake INIT, pick the cells you think the price will sweep through, and cash in at dynamic odds. It runs on Initia's Interwoven Rollup (evm-1), with wallet, bridging, and settlement all handled by InterwovenKit.

**The idea**

Most prediction markets are slow, clunky, and feel nothing like the markets they're tracking, which is the funniest part. So Griddy compresses speculation into a real-time arcade loop: BTC/USDT streams live from Binance into a log-scaled price × time grid, every cell is a bet, and odds update every second based on current distance-to-target and time remaining.

You're not holding a position. You're calling where the line goes next, and betting on the path it takes to get there.

**How it works**

1. Bridge INIT from Initia testnet L1 into evm-1 via the built-in InterwovenKit bridge modal
2. Hit *Deposit and Start*, sign one tx, you're in a session
3. Play. Click any future cell to place a bet at its live odds. Hundreds of bets per session, zero on-chain txs in between
4. Hit *End* and one withdrawal tx settles everything

Exactly two transactions per session: deposit + settle. Everything in between is pure frontend gameplay running on top of a real market feed.

**Why Initia**

Interwoven Rollups gave us a dedicated EVM chain where the settlement contract can live without fighting anyone for blockspace. InterwovenKit handles the entire wallet and bridge UX, so users show up with testnet INIT and are placing bets 30 seconds later, no chain-switching gymnastics. And because assets bridge in from the L1, judges (and eventually real users) get to pay with a familiar, liquid token instead of some weird wrapped thing.

**Tech snapshot**

React 19, Vite, wagmi + viem, @initia/interwovenkit-react, a custom probability/odds engine, canvas-rendered grid with a ring-buffer price history, and a real-time Binance WebSocket feed.
